$comment: First profile-mode scope contract. Seeded from profiles/expertmachina/scope-template.json, tightened against the ACTUAL ExpertMachina repo layout (inspected 2026-07-09). Globs are relative to targetRepo, not to AgenticTeam. SCOPING-ONLY pass: no Builder work is authorized until a human ratifies this contract.
objective: Create the Finance & Cost Leakage Workbench as the second commercial entry in Workbench Catalog v1: a governed bundle of declared skill contracts (workbench.yaml + skills/*.yaml + corpus + runner) that diagnoses cost leakage signals (duplicate/overlapping spend, contract-vs-invoice drift, unowned recurring costs, missing approval evidence, stale vendor terms) over a governed finance corpus. It follows the customer_operations pattern exactly: a consumer OUTSIDE the governed backend, using only existing doors (.empkg packages + MCP at a real AGENT token's clearance), with findings re-entering canon ONLY through the proposal lane (D29 One-Way Valve).
humanApproval: RATIFIED by Robert (robertkonecny@gmail.com) on 2026-07-09, in session: five skill contracts ratified with standing diagnostic-only policy, and branch precondition confirmed. Ratification record: handoffs/2026-07-09-ws-finance-cost-leakage-ratification.md

skillContracts.$comment: Ratified 2026-07-09. These five are the declared bundle; the Builder implements exactly these as skills/*.yaml contracts, no additions without re-scoping.

ratified.purpose: Finds likely duplicate invoices, payments, vendor charges, or recurring expenses.
Compares approved contract terms against invoice/payment evidence and flags mismatches.
Finds recurring subscriptions, vendors, tools, or services with unclear owner, approver, or business purpose.
Flags spend items where approval evidence is absent, stale, incomplete, or inaccessible.
Detects vendor contracts, pricing, renewal terms, or obligations that appear outdated or no longer aligned with current spend.

skillContracts.standingPolicy: These skills produce diagnostic findings only. They do not mutate canonical facts, vendor records, contracts, invoices, approvals, or audit state. Any suggested correction re-enters through the governed proposal lane.

preconditions: Builder may start only from up-to-date main after test/resurrect-golden-path-e2e lands or is explicitly abandoned/merged away — never on top of the test branch.
All five ratified skill contracts must be authored as skills/*.yaml and ACTIVE per Catalog v1 convention before the runner will accept them (non-ACTIVE contracts refused).
nonGoals: No changes to the governed backend (backend/app/**) — no new endpoints, projection types, connectors, policy, identity, or ledger logic
No changes to the MCP gateway or mcp_server.py
No new fact approval states, evidence classes, or clearance levels
No vault writes and no changes to vault managed folders or the proposal-lane floor (00_system, 07_agent_workspaces, 08_proposals)
No frontend changes in this workstream
No real customer financial data in the corpus — synthetic/example finance documents only, following the compliance_obligation corpus pattern
projectionOnlyRule: The workbench consumes ONLY projections and packages: .empkg via the package channel and MCP path/neighbor/subgraph queries at the AGENT token's clearance. It never reads backend internals, the ledger, or raw stores directly. If the workbench needs data no existing projection provides, that is a BLOCKED finding for the Steward — not a reason to reach deeper.
canonicalMutationPolicy: Zero direct canonical writes — constitutional, not configurable (D29). Workbench findings re-enter exclusively as proposal-lane submissions (vault 08_proposals -> PROPOSAL-lane connector -> CANDIDATE -> human gate -> DERIVED fact). Proposal-lane candidates are never auto-approved. The existing fifth permanent guard (backend/test_agent_authorship_guard.py) must still prove from the ledger alone that no agent principal wrote canonical facts.
auditProvenanceExpectations: Every finding the runner emits must carry evidence citations back to governed facts with source, ingestion date, and approval state, following the customer_operations evidence-driven kinding pattern (refusal-first, declared same-subject/cross-document evidence rules in each skill contract). Any consumption of packages/MCP appears in the ledger through existing mechanisms — the workstream adds no new audit machinery and bypasses none.
permissionBoundaryExpectations: The runner operates at a real AGENT token's clearance; all MCP/package reads are clearance-filtered by the existing engine. The acceptance test must include at least one denied-access case proving the workbench degrades to explicit refusal (not silent omission, not fabrication) when clearance excludes needed facts.
compileGateImpact: None permitted. backend/test_compile_gates.py and all permanent guards must pass unchanged. The new workbench must be structurally valid to the existing catalog convention (workbench.yaml + skills/*.yaml contracts) without modifying the runner-side validation it is checked against.

proofRequirements.mustShow: new corpus tests pass: corpus documents load, skill contracts parse and are ACTIVE, workbench.yaml is catalog-valid
each declared skill produces evidence-cited findings on the seeded corpus, refusal-first behavior on unsupported questions (profile rule 1)
explicit refusal/no-evidence output when the corpus lacks support — never fabricated findings (profile rule 1); at least one such case per skill contract
the existing permanent guard still proves from the ledger alone that no agent principal wrote canonical facts (profile rules 3-5, D29)
clearance filtering intact plus the workbench's denied-access case: explicit refusal under insufficient clearance (profile rule 7)
no regressions vs a baseline run recorded BEFORE the first Builder edit (baseline output pasted in Builder handoff)

verificationGate.$comment: Per profiles/expertmachina/workflows/evidence-gate.md. A provenance or permission fail is never waivable by vote count; it escalates to a human.

rollbackAnchor: git tag ws-finance-cost-leakage-baseline on the Builder's branch, created before the first edit
stopConditions: THIS PASS: Steward stops after this contract and its handoff note are written — no Builder work until humanApproval is updated from PENDING by Robert
Builder stops when all six proofs have run with literal evidence and the handoff (incl. profile addendum A1-A5) is written; does not merge
Builder stops immediately if any needed data lacks an existing projection/package door (BLOCKED finding to Steward)
Sweeper stops if the baseline suite is red
Any agent stops immediately if work requires editing a forbiddenGlob
Any agent stops immediately upon finding a write path from workbench code to canonical state (critical defect, straight to human)

downstreamPlan.part: workbench bundle: workbench.yaml, skill contracts, seeded synthetic corpus, runner (customer_operations as reference)
corpus + acceptance tests incl. refusal and denied-access cases

nextRecommendedAgent: human ratification first; then builder / opus
